requirement_groups.title: A2000 - Complete Requirements
requirement_groups.description: Complete all requirements for Bachelor of Arts

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete units as specified in Parts A, B and C (144 points): a minimum of 96 points of arts listed study over Part A and Part B, and 48 points of free electives in Part C.

To satisfy the requirements of the Bachelor of Arts you must complete 144 credit points as specified in the course requirements, including:

â¢ a maximum of 60 credit points at Level 1; and
â¢ a minimum of 36 credit points at Level 3, of which at least 24 credit points must be Arts units.

Units are 6 points unless otherwise specified.

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Specified studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete:

- 24 credit points of Discipline elective studies which can be combined into a minor in an area of study different to the chosen arts major from Part A.; and
- 24 credit points of units from the Professional Futures domains.

requirement_groups.title: Discipline elective studies
requirement_groups.description: You must complete 24 credit points of Discipline elective studies which can be combined into a minor of study different to the chosen major from Part A and with no more than 12 credit points at Level 1.

requirement_groups.title: Professional Futures
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 24 credit points from the units in the following Professional Futures domains.

Note: If you are enrolled in the double degree D3002 Bachelor of Education (Honours) in Secondary Education and Bachelor of Arts, you must complete 12 credit points of units from the Professional Futures domains and the remaining 12 credit points should be selected from level 1, 2 or 3 Arts' elective units.

For your discipline studies in the double degree secondary education specialisation, you must complete a Victorian Institute of Teaching minor study in one secondary teaching area. The minor study must consist of four units, with no more than two units at first year level. Space for the minor study units will come from elective unit allocations on the course map. Approved disciplines can be found on the secondary specialist areas page.
.

requirement_groups.title: Global immersion
requirement_groups.description:
You may complete the following units or any of the Arts study tours or semester length exchange or study abroad program.

Please visit https://www.monash.edu/arts/learning-abroad/global-study-tours for more information.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Free elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You may choose electives from units offered within the Bachelor of Arts, or any other Arts undergraduate course*. Using your electives in this way, may assist you to complete:
â¢ an extended Arts major
â¢ an additional Arts major or minors
â¢ an Arts Bachelor + Masters accelerated pathway
â¢ additional Arts global study tour opportunities
â¢ additional Arts industry experience opportunities

Note: no more than two units (12 credit points) can be credited towards two majors, or a major and a minor. The same credit points cannot be credited towards more than one minor.

You may also use your free electives to explore different disciplines and experiences offered from across Monash. This includes taking undergraduate units^, minors or majors offered by other Faculties* and participating in University programs* such as Flagship Rich Educational Experiences (FREE).

Elective units may be at any level ensuring that the course rules are satisfied for the Bachelor of Arts.

* subject to unit rules and enrolment restrictions
^ undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3; the level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code.

requirement_groups.title: Part A. Major studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete one of the following majors including:

â¢ a maximum of 12 credit points at Level 1; and
â¢ a minimum of 18 credit points at Level 3.

Note:
â¢ It is recommended that you complete a sequence of level 1 units in more than one Arts major area in order to have options for finalising your choice of major.

â¢ If you intend to complete the Economics major, the background knowledge and application of maths is required at the equivalent VCE Year 12 Higher level. You may have satisfied this by completing relevant prerequisite unit/s, or by studying relevant topics in your final years of secondary school or by successfully completing either ECC1550 Introduction to quantitative skills for social sciences or MTH1010 Functions and their applications, as a first-year elective

â¢ The European languages extended major requires entry level 2 intermediate in at least one of two language options.

â¢ The Psychology extended major (APAC accredited level 1 sequence of units) is only offered in:
- A2000 Bachelor of Arts; and
- S2006 Bachelor of Science and Bachelor of Arts.

â¢ If you intend to complete honours in your major area, you should check the required pre-requisites (e.g., specific units/credit points etc) to ensure you meet eligibility requirements.

requirement_groups.title: APAC  - Psychology extended  major
requirement_groups.description:
The Psychology extended major (APAC accredited level 1 sequence of units) is only offered in the single degree A2000 Bachelor of Arts or in the double degree S2006 Bachelor of Science and Bachelor of Arts.

If you are completing the Psychology extended major (APAC accredited level 1 sequence of units) below you will be required to complete 60 credit points of Psychology units and 36 credit points of Arts units which must include Arts electives (12 credit points) and Professional Futures domain units (24 credit points).

requirement_groups.title: European languages extended major
requirement_groups.description: You have the option of reaching proficient level in two European Languages by completing an extended major. You must begin at entry level 2 intermediate level in at least one of the two languages.

requirement_groups.title: Part E. Free elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of free elective study.

Units not completed in Part A, Part B, Part C and Part D are recommended as they enable you to further your discipline knowledge more broadly. Free elective units may be chosen from the faculty or across the University as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on admission to the units. The units may be at any level ensuring that the course rules are satisfied for the Bachelor of Global Studies.

You can enrich your degree by using some of your free electives to take a study tour, field trip or exchange opportunity. You can also use this space to hone your academic and professional skills with an internship or a range of practical units. See Arts enrichment and Monash Intercultural Studies list of units to discover the breadth of options available.

The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code; undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3. You may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties.

If you are enrolled in a double degree course, some units required for the other degree are credited as electives towards the global studies degree.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Discipline elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 30 credit points from one or more discipline elective areas listed below.

Note: You may also take units in Part A, Part B or Part C not already completed.

requirement_groups.title: Sustainability electives
requirement_groups.description: Explore global environmental challenges and solutions through units on climate history, urban sustainability, and the intersection of climate change with social justice. Evaluate the past, understand the present, and design a sustainable future for our planet.

requirement_groups.title: Research pathway
requirement_groups.description: This unit is recommended if you are interested in pursuing research related to global studies after graduating.

requirement_groups.title: Global communication electives
requirement_groups.description: Understand how language shapes our engagement with a diverse and interconnected world. Develop the strategies and skills of communication needed to thrive in internationalised workplaces.

requirement_groups.title: Culture and identity electives
requirement_groups.description: Explore how social identities are formed through global circulation of cultural beliefs and practices. Understand the role of culture in creating meaningful, and sometimes contested, worldviews.

requirement_groups.title: Social justice electives
requirement_groups.description: Tackle critical issues of power, and inequality through an exploration of historical and contemporary struggles against racism, colonialism, and economic disparity. Understand the roots of injustice and contribute to a more equitable world.

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
The course comprises 144 credit points, of which 96 credit points are global studies and 48 credit points are free electives.

To satisfy the requirements of the Bachelor of Global Studies you must complete 144 credit points as specified in the course requirements, including:

- a maximum of 60 credit points at Level 1 and,Â 
- a minimum of 36 credit points at Level 3, of which 24 credit points must be from Part A, B, C and D.

Note 1. Units are 6 credit points unless otherwise stated.
Note 2. The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code.

requirement_groups.title: A2002 - Complete Requirements
requirement_groups.description: Complete all requirements for Bachelor of Media Communication

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
The course comprises 144 credit points, of which 96 credit points are media communication studies and 48 credit points are free electives.

To satisfy the requirements of the Bachelor of Media Communication you must completeÂ 144 credit points as specified in the course requirements, including:

- a maximum of 60 credit points at Level 1; andÂ 
- a minimum of 30 credit points at Level 3, of which at least 18 credit points must be from the specialisation.

Note 1. Units are 6 credit points unless otherwise stated.
Note 2. The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code.
Note 3. You must complete 24 credit points of Level 1 foundation units in the first year of study, which represents the Level 1 sequences for two specialisations. You are required to nominate your chosen specialisation(s) at the end of your first year of study.
Note 4. If you choose to complete two specialisations, you are required to complete a minimum of 36 credit points in each specialisation.

A unit cannot be counted towards two specialisations. If you choose to complete the following specialisation combinations, you must complete the alternative capstone units as specified in the specialisation requirements.

Public Relations and Media specialisations:

- ATS3992 is listed as a capstone in both the Public relations and Media specialisations. This unit cannot be counted as meeting capstone requirements for both specialisations.

Journalism and Public Relations specialisations:

- ATS3645 is listed as a capstone in both the Journalism and Public relations specialisations. This unit cannot be counted as meeting capstone requirements for both specialisations.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Specialist studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete the units (36 credit points) in your chosen specialisation.

Note 1. It is recommended that you complete level 1 unit sequences first as these lay the foundations for level 2 and level 3 specialisation units.
Note 2. If you are undertaking a single degree, you may complete two specialisations by replacing 36 credit points from Part D. with the required Specialist units. Consultation with the Course Director is advised.

requirement_groups.title: Part A. Core studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 36 credit points from the following list, comprising:
- ATS1119 and ATS1206,
- 12 credit points of Level 2 units; and
- 12 credit points of Level 3 units.

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Foundation studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete two units (12 credit points) from two of the following listed specialisations, to total 24 credit points.

Note: The units from this component of the course will be credited to your chosen specialisation in Part C as they represent the foundation knowledge for your specialist studies.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Free electives studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of free elective study.

Units not completed in Part B or Part C. are recommended as they enable you to further your discipline knowledge more broadly. Free elective units may be chosen from the faculty or across the University as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on admission to the units. The units may be at any level ensuring that the course rules are satisfied for the Bachelor of Media and Communication.

You can enrich your degree by using some of your free electives to take a study tour, field trip or exchange opportunity. You can also use this space to hone your academic and professional skills with an internship or a range of practical units. See Arts enrichment and Monash Intercultural Lab list of units to discover the breadth of options available.

The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code; undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3. You may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties.

If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the other degree are credited as electives towards the media communications specialist degree.

requirement_groups.description: Complete all requirements for Bachelor of Music

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Context studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 36 credit points, comprising:
- 30 credit points (five units) of Context studies; and
- 6 credit points (one unit) of Specified context electives.

requirement_groups.title: Specified context electives
requirement_groups.description: You must complete one of the following units (6 credit points) that you have not already completed in your studies.

requirement_groups.title: Context studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete the 30 credit points (five units) from the following list.

Note: You can only complete one of ATS2060 or ATS2307 as a Context studies unit.

requirement_groups.title: Part A. Specialist studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete one specialisation from the following.

To avoid exceeding the standard three year course duration you must enrol in the music specialist study 1 and 2 units in your first year of study, then progress to the music specialist study 3 to 6 units in the following years. You must pass each unit sequentially.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Free elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of free elective studies.

You may select units from
- Part C. Context studies
- the music electives listed below,
- across the Arts faculty, or from across the University (including a major or minor from another course).
Note: You need to have met any prerequisites and ensure there are no restrictions on admission to the units you choose. You may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties. The units may be at any level ensuring that the course rules are satisfied for the Bachelor of Music.

You can enrich your degree by using some of your free electives to take a study tour, field trip or exchange opportunity. requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of free elective study.

Elective units may be chosen from units available in the Bachelor of Arts and Social Sciences course including to extend your major or to complete a second major or minor(s) from the course. Note, a maximum of 12 credit points may be counted towards two majors or a major and a minor.

Elective units may also be taken from non-arts disciplines to broaden your knowledge or to complete a major or minor(s) from another course as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment in the units. The units may be at any level ensuring that the course rules are satisfied for the Bachelor of Arts and Social Sciences.

requirement_groups.title: Research pathway (optional)
requirement_groups.description:
You can complete a research pathway in your course if:

- you have achieved a minimum weighted average mark of 80% in the units of this course;
- you have the permission of the Course Director; and
- a suitable thesis supervisor is available.

If you are admitted into the course at entry Level 1 or 2, the following unit replacements will apply:
- APG5084 Designing research as offered in Part C.
- APG5849 Research thesis A replaces 6 credit points from Part D.
- APG5850 Research thesis B replaces 12 credit points from Part B.

If you are admitted into the course at entry Level 3, the replacement units will be determined based on previous study in consultation with the Course Director.

Note 1. APG5084 Designing research is a co-requisite for enrolment in APG5849 Research thesis A and must be completed in the same semester.
Note 2. Undertaking a research pathway does not guarantee entry into a graduate research degree (e.g. Masters by Research, PhD).

You must complete 24 credit points from the following list:

requirement_groups.title: Monash and SJTU students
requirement_groups.description:
Monash students usually commence the first year at Monash and the second year at Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU). Following the completion of the Master of International Relations studies (Part A and B totalling 48 credit points), you will travel to SJTU, to complete broadening Master of Arts in Chinese politics and economy course studies (48 credit points); the thesis will take place at the end of the second year at SJTU.

SJTU students usually commence the first year at SJTU. On the completion of Master of Arts in Chinese politics and economy coursework units, you will travel to Monash to complete Master of International Relations studies (Part A and B totalling 48 credit points). Having completed the Master of International Relations requirements, you will return to SJTU to complete thesis requirements.

requirement_groups.title: Information for Monash and SJTU students
requirement_groups.description:
Monash students will normally commence the first year at Monash (in July), and the second year at SJTU.

Following completion of the academic program at Monash comprising 48 credit points (one year full-time) of study towards the Master of Strategic Communications Management, selected students will travel to China to complete a further 48 credit points (one year full-time) study toward theÂ Master of Journalism and Communication from Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU). The thesis will take place at the end of the second year at SJTU (normally around early June).Â 

SJTU students will normally commence the first year at SJTU, and the second year at Monash (July). They will then return to SJTU to complete the requirements of the thesis.Â

requirement_groups.title: Research pathway (Optional)
requirement_groups.description:
You can complete a research pathway in your course if:

- you are undertaking the policy studies specialisation;
- you have achieved a minimum weighted average mark of 80% in the units of this course;
- you have the permission of the Course Director; and
- a suitable thesis supervisor is available.

If you are admitted into the course at entry level 1, the following unit replacements will apply:
- ATI5084 Designing research replaces 6 credit points from Part C.
- ATI5849 Research thesis A replaces 6 credit points from Part C.
- ATI5850 Research thesis B replaces 12 credit points from Part B.

If you are admitted into the course at entry level 2, the replacement units will be determined based on previous study in consultation with the Course Director.

Note 1. ATI5084 Designing research is a pre-requisite for enrolment in APG5849 Research thesis A.
Note 2. Undertaking the research pathway does not guarantee entry into a graduate research degree (e.g. Masters by Research, PhD).

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description: In choosingÂ your units,Â you must ensure thatÂ you complete no more than 10 units (60 credit points) at Level 1 and at leastÂ six units (36 credit points) at Level 3, including four units (24 credit points) at Level 3 offered by the Faculty of Business and Economics.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of elective study. Electives may be selected from within the Bachelor of Business. These may also be used to complete a second major (as listed above) or one or more minors (as listed below).

Electives can also be chosen from across the University, including completing a major or minor from another course if you have met the required prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolling in the units. You should also be aware of travel and timetabling limitations.

Notes:
-Enrolment in Faculty of Business and Economics units at campuses other than the campus of enrolment is subject to there being capacity after students from that campus have enrolled.

-If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the other degree are credited as electives towards this Business degree.

-You can enrich your degree to hone your academic and professional skills with a range of Flagship Rich Educational Experiences (FREE), including the Research, Experimentation and Discovery, Monash Innovation Guarantee, Global Immersion Guarantee, or Volunteering if not already completed as a specified business elective in Part A. These flagship rich educational experience units may be credited utilising your elective studies if approved by the faculty. There are both 6 and 12 credit point unit options available.

requirement_groups.title: Part A. Core studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points comprising:
- 42 credit points (seven units) from the following list; and
- 6 credit points (one unit) from specified business electives.

Notes:
- You must complete either ACF1100 or ACF1001 (one accounting unit).
- If you intend to complete a major in Accounting, you must complete ACF1100.

requirement_groups.title: Specified business elective
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 6 credit points (one unit) from the following list, unless you are undertaking the Bachelor of Education (Honours) in Secondary Education and Bachelor of Business (course D3007). If you are undertaking this course, EDF2078 Creative and critical teaching meets this requirement, and you are required to choose another 6 credit point unit from the Accounting, Business law, Business management or Economics and business strategy majors.

Notes:
- If you are considering BEX2421 or BEX2422, you may only choose to complete one of the two units.

- If you choose a 12 credit point unit you will need to reduce the amount of points available for elective studies in Part D by 6 credit points, which may impact onÂ the ability to complete a major using electives.

- If you are enrolled in a double degree it may be possible to do the Global Immersion Guarantee (12 credit points). For information on eligible double degree combinations please see Global Immersion Guarantee.

- If you are enrolled in the double degree B2051Â Bachelor of Digital Business and Bachelor of Business, youÂ cannot complete BEX2000 as a business specified elective in Part A, as the unit is a prohibition of core unit BEX3701 which must be completed in the Digital Business component.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Capstone studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete one unit (6, 12 or 24 credit points) from the Facultyâs capstone portfolio of units, which includes capstone units, consulting projects, international experiences and internships (industry placements).

Notes:
- If you choose a 12 or 24 credit point unit you will need to reduce the amount of points available for electives in Part D which may impact on the ability to complete a major using electives.

- If you are undertaking the Bachelor of Education (Honours) in Secondary Education and Bachelor of Business (course D3007), EDF4068 Teaching performance assessment meets this requirement, and you are required to choose another 6 credit point unit from the Accounting, Business law, Business management or Economics and business strategy majors.

- If you are completing a major in Accounting and seeking professional accounting accreditation you will need to complete ACX3900. You can undertake more than one unit from the following lists, which will count towards your electives.

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Major studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete at least one major from the list below. The major will comprise 42Â credit points from Part B. Major studies plus 6 credit points from Part A. Core studies to total 48 credit points which is required to equal a major.

Notes:

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description: In choosing your units, you must ensure that you complete no more than 10 units (60 credit points) at Level 1 and at least six units (36 credit points) at Level 3, including four units (24 credit points) at Level 3 offered by the Faculty of Business and Economics.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Capstone studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete one unit (6, 12 or 24 credit points) from the Facultyâs capstone portfolio of unitsÂ which includes capstone units, consulting projects, international experiences and internships (industry placements).

Notes:
- If you choose a 12 or 24 credit point unit it will reduce the amount of points available for electives in Part D which may impact on your ability to complete a major using electives.

requirement_groups.title: Part A. Core studies
requirement_groups.description:

requirement_groups.title: Specified commerce elective
requirement_groups.description:

You can enrich your degree to hone your academic and professional skills with a range of Flagship Rich Educational Experiences (FREE), including the Research, Experimentation and Discovery, Monash Innovation Guarantee, Global Immersion Guarantee and Volunteering. These flagship rich educational experience units may be credited in place of your specified commerce elective if approved by the faculty, or alternatively utilising your elective studies in Part D. There are both 6 and 12 credit point unit options available.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of elective study. Electives may be selected from within the Bachelor of Commerce. These may also be used to complete a second major (as listed above) or one or more minors (as listed below).Â 

Elective units can also be chosen from across the University, including to complete a major or minor from another course, as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolling in the units. You should also be aware of travel and timetabling limitations.

Notes:
- Enrolment in Faculty of Business and Economics units at campuses other than the campus of enrolment is subject to there being capacity after students from that campus have enrolled.

- If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the other degree are credited as electives towards this Commerce degree.

If you undertake both the Actuarial Studies specialisation (within the Actuarial Science component) and the Econometrics major, you cannot select ETC3460, as this unit is completed in Part C Specialist Studies â Actuarial Studies specialisation within the Actuarial Science component. Additionally, you may not select ETC2440, ETC2520, or ETC3550 from the Econometrics major, as these units are completed in Part B Discipline Studies within the Actuarial Science component.

If you undertake both the Actuarial Studies specialisation (within the Actuarial Science component) and the Mathematical Foundations of Econometrics major (within the Commerce component), you cannot select ETC3430 or ETC3460 in the major, as these units are completed as part of the Actuarial Science component.

If you undertake the Mathematical foundations of econometrics major you cannot not complete ETC2440 as you will undertake this unit in Part B Discipline studies within the Actuarial Science component. Instead, you must complete both ETC3410 and ETC3450 from the additional core units of the major.

Where you have space in your degree you may complete a second major with no more than two units contributing towards both of your chosen majors or towards both a major and the specialisation.

You can enrich your degree to hone your academic and professional skills with a range of flagship rich experiences, including the Research, Experimentation and Discovery, Monash Innovation Guarantee and Global Immersion Guarantee

Enrolment in Faculty of Business and Economics units at campuses other than the campus of enrolment is subject to there being capacity after students from that campus have enrolled.

Electives can also be chosen from across the University, including to complete a major or minor from another course if you have met the required prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolling in the units. You should also be aware of travel and timetabling limitations.

Notes:

- The Research pathway in the Master of Accounting is available by application only. The Course Director will invite you (via email) to apply for the Research pathway prior to November each year.
- To be eligible for the Research Pathway you must have completed ACF5100, ACF5120, ACF5150, and ACF5330 with an overall Weighted Average Mark (WAM) of at least 70, and no mark below 60 in any of these units.
- If you are accepted into the Research pathway, you must complete the requirements of that pathway, which include the Specified Honours research studies and the Specified discipline studies. These units replace ACF5080, ACF5130, ACF5320 and the Capstone unit in the Coursework pathway.

B6060.course_title: Bachelor of Actuarial Science and Master of Actuarial Studies

requirement_groups.title: B6060 - Complete Requirements
requirement_groups.description: Complete all requirements for Bachelor of Actuarial Science and Master of Actuarial Studies

requirement_groups.title: Stage 1. Bachelor of Actuarial Science
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete units as specified in Parts A, B, C and D (144 credit points).

In choosing your units, you must ensure that you complete no more than 10 units (60 credit points) at Level 1 and at least six units (36 credit points) at Level 3, including four units (24 credit points) at Level 3 offered by the Faculty of Business and Economics.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of elective studies, comprising:

- 24 credit points from Electives; and
- 24 credit points from Specified discipline electives.

requirement_groups.title: Electives
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 24 credit points of elective studies.

You must complete at least six units (36 credit points) at Level 3 in this course. If you take less than six Level 3 units in total under Part B and Part C, you must take additional Level 3 units in Part D. Electives can also be chosen from across the University, including to complete a major or minor from another course if you have met the required prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolling in the units. Enrolment in Faculty of Business and Economics units at campuses other than the campus of enrolment is subject to there being capacity after students from that campus have enrolled. You should also be aware of travel and timetabling limitations.

No more than two units can contribute towards two majors, or a major and a minor, in the same course (including a double degree course). The same unit is not credited towards two minors.

Electives can be identified using the advanced search in the current edition of the Handbook. Majors and minors can be identified using the Handbook advanced search. The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code; undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3.

You can enrich your degree to hone your academic and professional skills with a range of flagship rich experiences, including the Research, Experimentation and Discovery, Monash Immersion Guarantee, Global Immersion Guarantee or Volunteering. These flagship rich experience units may be credited in place of your electives if approved by the faculty. There are both 6 and 12 credit point unit options available.

Note: If you are in the double degree course with the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) completing the software engineering specialisation, you should replace FIT2094 with a FIT-coded level 2 elective.

requirement_groups.title: Professional placement
requirement_groups.description:
FIT3045 is only available if you are selected to participate in the Industry Based Learning placement program. [https://www.monash.edu/it/industry-based-learning]

If you are selected for the IBL program you will complete FIT3045 Industry-based learning (18 credit points) and FIT2108 Industry-based learning (0 points); or FIT3045 (18 credit points), FIT2108 (0 points) and either FIT3201 Industry-based learning onboarding (8 weeks) (0 points) or FIT3202 Industry-based learning onboarding (4 weeks) (0 points) (depending on the duration of your placement). The IBL placement will replace your Applied studies project units and the nominated Level 3 unit in your major.

In the IBL program you must complete a unit over summer semester or overload in one semester by one unit in order to complete the degree within three years (this is because you only complete 18 credit points of credit during the IBL placement semester). You must have completed at least three semesters of your course before starting your IBL placement.

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
In choosing units you must ensure that you complete no more than 10 Level one units (60 credit points), and at least six units (36 credit points) at Level 3 of which at least four (24 credit points) are Bachelor of Information Technology listed (FIT-coded) units.

Note: If you are in a double degree with the Bachelor of Information Technology, no more than 36 credit points can be completed at Level 1.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Free elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
Note 1: Your elective units may be chosen from any of the units offered at Monash to suit your interests or career aspirations. You may choose to take other units available in the Bachelor of Information Technology course to extend your major or to complete a second major or minor(s) from the course, or you may take Faculty of IT units from C2001 Bachelor of Computer Science, or the software engineering specialisation in the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours).

Elective units may also be used to sample from across the University or to complete a major or minor(s) from another course asÂ long asÂ you have completedÂ the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment in the units. The units may be at any level, however, no more than 10 units (60 credit points) at Level 1 may be credited to the Bachelor of Information Technology and a minimum 36 credit points must be at Level 3.

Note 2: If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the partner degree are credited as electives towards this degree.

requirement_groups.title: Discipline elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
To enhance your IT studies the Faculty recommends the following minors and elective units which can be taken as part of your free electives. Other IT minors are also available, check the Faculty Handbook pages.

Note: If you are doing the Applied cybersecurity major you cannot do the Cybersecurity minor.

requirement_groups.title: Part E. Free elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of free electives from across the university, provided you satisfy the unit rules, including choosing to complete a major or minor from other courses.

The units may be at any level, however, no more than 10 units (60 credit points) at Level 1 may be credited to the Bachelor of Computer Science and a minimum 36 credit points must be at Level 3.

If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the partner degree are credited as electives towards the computer science degree

requirement_groups.title: Discipline elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
If you are studying at the Clayton campus you can choose to enhance your IT studies with the following recommended minors and elective units from the Faculty of Information Technology which can be taken as part of your free electives. Other IT minors are also available, check the Faculty Handbook pages.

Note: If you are undertaking the Cybersecurity specialisation you cannot do the Cybersecurity minor.

requirement_groups.title: Part A. Foundation studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete the following units.

Note: If you are in the double degree course with the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) completing the software engineering specialisation you must replace FIT2094 with a FIT-coded Level 2 elective unit.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Applied studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete one of the following options.

Clayton students - you complete a project studies (12 credit points) relevant to your specialisation; or the industry-based learning placement (18 credit points).

Malaysia students - you complete a project studies (12 credit points) relevant to your specialisation plus the industry-based learning placement (18 credit points) or the industry work experience unit (0 credit points).

requirement_groups.title: Industry based learning placement
requirement_groups.description:
FIT3045 is only available if you are selected to participate in the IBL placement program.

For Clayton students - If you are selected to participate in the IBL placement program you will complete FIT3045 Industry-based learning (18 credit points) and FIT2108 Industry-based learning (0 credit points); or FIT3045, FIT2108 and one of FIT3201 Industry-based learning onboarding (8 weeks) (0 credit points) or FIT3202 Industry-based learning onboarding (4 weeks) (0 credit points) (depending on the duration of your placement). This will replace the Applied studies project units and the Level 3 elective unit in your specialisation.

For Malaysia students - Students at the Malaysia campus are required to have completed an industry placement in order to graduate as required by the Malaysian Qualifications Agency. This can either be FIT3045 Industry based learning (if you are selected to participate) or FIT3199 Industry work experience. Note: You must complete FIT3199 over the Summer vacation period between second and third year.

If you are selected to participate in the IBL placement program you will complete FIT3045 Industry-based learning (18 credit points) which will replace the Level 3 elective unit (6 credit points) in your specialisation and two free electives (12 credit points).

All students in the IBL program must complete a unit over summer semester or overload in one semester by one unit in order to complete the degree within three years (this is because you only complete 18 credit points of credit during the IBL placement semester). You must have completed at least three semesters of your course before starting your IBL placement.

requirement_groups.title: Information Technology  component
requirement_groups.description: You must complete 96 credit points, including all of the requirements in Part A and Part B, for the Bachelor of Information Technology. No more than 36 credit points can be completed at Level 1.

requirement_groups.title: Science component
requirement_groups.description:
In choosing the 96 credit points of Bachelor of Science listed study over Part A and Part B, you must ensure that you complete six science listed units (36 credit points) at Level 1, and ten science listed units (60 credit points) at Levels 2 and 3 with a minimum of four (24 credit points) at Level 3.

You must complete the mathematics and statistics unit requirement within the first 72 credit points of this double degree course.

The psychology extended major is not available in this double degree.

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Major studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete the software development major.

A major requires eight units with no more than two units at level one (12 credit points) and at least three units (18 credit points) at level three.Â

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of elective units.

Electives can be chosen from across theÂ Monash Indonesia campus, as long as you have met the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment in the units. Please note that completion of the following Innovation and Leadership units will allow you to complete a second major.

If you are in a double degree course you don't complete Part C. Some units required for the other degree will be credited as electives towards this degree.

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
Note 1: To progress to Honours you are required to maintain at least a distinction average (70%)Â throughout your course. If you do not meet this standard you will be required to transfer to C2001 Bachelor of Computer Science or C2000 Bachelor of Information Technology.

Note 2: No more than 10 Level one units (60 credit points); and at least six Level 3 (or higher) units (36 credit points), must be completed.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Applied studies
requirement_groups.description: You must complete a full-year project (12 credit points) relevant to your specialisation, or the industry-based learning unit (18 credit points).

requirement_groups.title: Industry based learning placement
requirement_groups.description:
FIT3045 is available only if you are selected to participate in the IBL placement program.

If you are selected to participate in the IBL placement program you will complete FIT3045 Industry-based learning (18 credit points) and FIT2108 Industry-based learning (0 credit points); or FIT3045, FIT2108 and one of FIT3201 Industry-based learning onboarding (8 weeks) (0 credit points) or FIT3202 Industry-based learning onboarding (4 weeks) (0 credit points) (depending on the duration of your placement). This will replace the Applied studies project units and the Level 3 elective unit in your specialisation.

All students in the IBL program must complete a unit over summer semester or overload in one semester by one unit in order to complete the degree within four years (this is because you only complete 18 credit points of credit during the IBL placement semester). You must have completed at least three semesters of your course before starting your IBL placement.

requirement_groups.title: Research pathway
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete the following units.

Note 1: Enrolment in the research units is dependent on available supervisors and projects. Eligible students will be ranked based on their entire academic record and assessed for suitability to undertake the research component of this program.

Note 2: To be eligible for the research pathway:

You must have successfully completed at least 24 credit points of level 5 FIT-coded units; and have an overall average of at least 80% across all Level 5 units; and must have have achieved at least a distinction (75%) in FIT5125 IT research methods; and achieved an overall course WAM of 70%.

If you have a WAM between 75-79% across all Level 5 units you must have successfully completed at least 24 credit points of level 5 FIT-coded units; and demonstrated research capability with written support from a prospective supervisor; and must have achieved at least a distinction (75%) in FIT5125 IT research methods; and have achieved an overall course WAM of 70%.

requirement_groups.title: Secondary education double degree courses
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete the following units (12 credit points) and two units to commence a first discipline*** (12 credit points) and two units to commence a second discipline*** (12 credit points).

***These two pairs of discipline units will determine the two subject areas you will go on to qualify in to teach at secondary school level once you are a registered teacher.

Discipline sequences of study are chosen from the partner degree of the destination double degree from the following areas: accounting, biology, business management, chemistry, drama, economics, English as an additional language (EAL), English, general science, geography, health, history, information and communication technology, languages, legal studies, mathematics, media studies, music, physics, physical education, social education, and fine art.

A link to the secondary specialist/discipline areas, prerequisites and units is available in the Note above.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
The identified elective units complement the broad range of skills and knowledge employed by Educational Designers. Some units may have enrolment rules such as pre-requisites and you may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties.

The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code; undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3. If you wish to pursue an alternative elective unit, you will need to seek approval from the Faculty of Education.

You can enrich your degree by honing your academic and professional skills through a range of flagship rich educational experiences (FREE), including the Monash Innovation Guarantee. These FREE units may be credited in place of your free electives if approved by the faculty. You must discuss your eligibility with your Education Success Adviser (ESA) prior to enrolling in FREE. There are both 6 and 12 credit point unit options available.

From the following elective unit selections, you must complete 30 credit points of which no more than 12 credit points are at Level 1.

requirement_groups.title: Arts component
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 96 credit points comprising all the requirements from Parts A and B as described in the Bachelor of Arts degree.
At least 24 credit points must be completed at Level 3.

For Part B Arts Specified Study, you must complete 12 credit points of units from the Professional Futures domains and the remaining 12 credit points should be selected from Level 1, 2 or 3 Arts' elective units.

For your discipline studies in the double degree secondary education specialisation, you must complete a Victorian Institute of Teaching minor study in one secondary teaching area. The minor study must consist of four units, with no more than two units at first year level. Space for the minor study units will come from elective unit allocations on the course map. Approved disciplines can be found on the secondary specialist areas page: https://www.monash.edu/education/students/courses/units/secondary-specialisms

requirement_groups.title: Science component
requirement_groups.description:
In choosing the 96 credit points of Bachelor of Science listed study over Part A and Part B, you must ensure that you complete six science listed units (36 credit points) at Level 1 and ten science listed units (60 credit points) at Levels 2 and 3 with a minimum of four (24 credit points) at Level 3.

In selecting your science studies, you must take note of the specialisms required for teaching. Students in the secondary education specialisation must qualify for two separate specialist teaching areas. The secondary specialist areas available by course shows which secondary teaching areas are available. You must complete the mathematics and statistics unit requirement within the first 72 credit points of this double degree course.

The psychology extended major is not available in this double degree.

requirement_groups.title: Research pathway
requirement_groups.description:
A pathway to graduate research (e.g. master by research, PhD) is available if you have been admitted at Level 1 or Level 2 and have achieved a weighted average mark of at least 75% in the units of this course.

This pathway is only available to students enrolled at Clayton, and is not available at the point of course entry; if you wish to complete the research pathway units, you must enrol in the units below. Studentsâ eligibility will be checked prior to the commencement of EDF5613.

If you are commencing at entry Level 1 or 2, you must take the required two units in Part C of your studies.

Due to professional recognition requirements, the research pathway is not available if you have been admitted at Level 3. If you have been admitted at Level 3 and are interested in a research pathway you are encouraged to consider the Graduate Certificate of Educational Research.

requirement_groups.title: Option 2 - Pathway to graduate research
requirement_groups.description:
A pathway to graduate research (e.g. master by research, PhD) is available if you:

- have been admitted at level 1 or 2*; and
- achieve a weighted average mark (WAM) of at least 75% in the units of this course.

Students completing this pathway will need to make the following unit substitutions:

- EDF5613 Research approaches in education (12 credit points) to replace EDF5697 (6 points) and EDF5698 (6 points) (Part C)
- EDF5614 Research project in education (12 credit points) to replace 12 points of approved level 5 units from selected Faculty of Education graduate coursework programs (Part C)
- You are not required to takeÂ EDF5709 Professional placement - teaching (0 points) or EDF5710 Professional placement - observation (0 points) if you are completing the research pathway

Please note:

- this pathway is not available at the point of entry
- your eligibility to undertake this pathway will be checked prior to the commencement of EDF5613;
- if you are commencing at entry level 1 or 2, you must take the required two units in Part C of your studies.

*Â The research pathway is not available if you have been admitted at level 3. If you have been admitted at level 3 and are interested in a research pathway you are encouraged to consider the Graduate Certificate of Educational Research.

requirement_groups.title: Pathway to graduate research (optional)
requirement_groups.description:
A pathway to graduate research (e.g. master's by research, PhD) is available if you: have been admitted at level 1 or 2; and achieve a weighted average mark (WAM) of at least 75% in the units of this course.

If you are completing this pathway, you will need to make the following unit substitutions: EDF5613 Research approaches in education (12 credit points) to replace EDF5690 Educational leadership inquiry (12 credit points) (Part B); EDF5614 Research project in education (12 credit points) to replace 12 credit points of approved Level 5 units from selected Faculty of Education graduate coursework programs (Part C).

Please note: This pathway is not available at the point of entry; your eligibility to undertake this pathway will be checked prior to the commencement of EDF5613; if you are commencing at entry level 1 or 2, you must take the required two units in the final semester of your studies; and the research pathway is not available if you have been admitted at level 3. If you have been admitted at level 3 and are interested in a research pathway you are encouraged to consider the Graduate Certificate of Educational Research.

If you are completing this pathway, you must complete the following units.

requirement_groups.title: Engineering fundamentals
requirement_groups.description: You must complete the following units

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
The course comprises 192 credit points, of which 144 credit points are from core engineering studies and 48 credit points are available for elective studies.

The structure of this course includes a common first year for all students which is represented by Part A. Engineering fundamentals and design studies and B. Breadth studies

- No more than 60 credit points of Level 1 units can be credited to the engineering course.
- A minimum of 72 credit points must be completed in engineering studies at Levels 3, 4 or 5.

You must satisfy the continuous professional development requirements (See ENG0001 or ENG0002).

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Breadth studies
requirement_groups.description:
Engineering single degree - You must complete one unit (6 credit points) chosen from the First Year engineering breadth study list AND two free open electives (12 credit points).

You must complete foundational study, if required. If no foundational study is required, you may choose to select your free electives from the First Year engineering breadth study list or from across the University, subject to meeting the unit enrolment rules.

Engineering double degrees - You must complete one unit (6 credit points) chosen from the First Year engineering breadth study list.

If you are required to complete foundational study, the foundation unit is undertaken in place of the First Year engineering breadth unit. If you require both foundation units, the second foundation unit will increase your total credit points needed for the double degree by 6 credit points. A study overload will occur in Year 1 or 2.

requirement_groups.title: First year engineering breadth studies
requirement_groups.description:
Note 1. Biomedical engineering: If you intend to specialise in biomedical engineering, you must select and complete BMS1021.

Note 2. Software engineering: If you intend to specialise in software engineering, you must select and complete FIT1045, as it provides essential grounding in programming and prepares you for advanced software engineering units.

Note 3. CHM1011 and CHM1051 are mutual prohibition units, meaning that you can only complete either CHM1011 or CHM1051.

Note 4. If you complete a unit from this list that is also a core unit in your chosen specialisation, it will count towards that specialisation. You must still fulfil the First Year engineering breadth study requirement by completing another breadth unit.

requirement_groups.title: Foundational studies
requirement_groups.description:
Foundational studies - 12 credit points

If you have not completed either or both VCE Physics (Units 3 and 4) and VCE Specialist mathematics (Units 3 and 4) or equivalent and with the required study score, you must complete the following physics and/or mathematics foundation units.

To check the required study score, visit https://www.monash.edu/engineering/current-students/enrolment-and-re-enrolment/how-to-enrol-and-re-enrol/course-information/undergraduate-enrolment

requirement_groups.title: Parts C, D and E. Specialist studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete Parts C, D and E of one of the specialisations that are offered on your campus of study.

Note: Aerospace Engineering and Environmental Engineering are not offered at Malaysia

Please be aware that certain specialisations have specific engineering unit requirements to meet the accreditation standards set by the Engineering Accreditation Council Malaysia (EAC) and/or the Board of Engineers Malaysia (BEM). It is important to note these requirements and ensure that you complete the necessary engineering units for your chosen specialisation to satisfy the accreditation criteria.

requirement_groups.title: Engineering minors
requirement_groups.description:
An engineering minor will diversify your skill set and add versatility. If you are enrolled in the engineering single degree course, you can use your engineering electives in Years 2, 3 and 4 to complete a minor from a different engineering discipline.

While most specialisations have complementary combinations with minors, it should be noted that the Biomedical Engineering and Software Engineering specialisations currently do not offer any options to pursue a minor. Minors are also not available in the engineering double-degrees.

all_units.MTE2201: Plastics and the planet: Health, impact and sustainability
all_units.CHE1010: Grand challenges in chemical engineering: Delivering sustainable food, water and energy

requirement_groups.title: E3002 - Complete Requirements
requirement_groups.description: Complete all requirements for Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) and Bachelor of Arts

requirement_groups.title: Engineering component
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 144 credit points comprising all of Part A, six credit points from Part B, all of Part C and all of Part D as described in the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours).

The Common First Year consists of Parts A and B.

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Breadth studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 6 credit points (one unit) from either Foundational studies or First year engineering breadth studies.

Note 1: If you have not completed either or both VCE Physics (Units 3 and 4) and VCE Specialist mathematics (Units 3 and 4) or equivalent and with the required study score, you must complete the related foundational studies unit(s) in place of the First year engineering breadth studies.

Note 2: If you require both foundation units, the second foundation unit will increase your total credit points needed for the double degree by 6 credit points. A study overload will occur in Year 1 or 2.

requirement_groups.title: First year engineering breadth studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 6 credit points (one unit) from the following list.

Note 1: If you are required to complete one, or both, of the foundational studies units, you are not required to complete 6 credit points of First year engineering breadth studies.

Note 2. Biomedical engineering: If you intend to specialise in biomedical engineering, you must select and complete BMS1021.

Note 3. Chemical engineering: Due to the prohibition with CHE2161, you are strongly advised against completing MEC2404 as a First Year elective. This choice may result in an inadequate foundation for the subsequent core unit CHE3167 in the chemical engineering specialisation.

Note 4. Software engineering: If you intend to specialise in software engineering, you must select and complete FIT1045, as it provides essential grounding in programming and prepares you for advanced software engineering units.

Note 5. CHM1011 and CHM1051 are mutual prohibition units, meaning that you can only complete either CHM1011 or CHM1051.

Note 6. If you complete a First Year engineering breadth unit that happens to be a core unit in your chosen specialisation, the unit will count towards your specialisation study. You must still fulfil the First Year engineering breadth study requirement by completing another breadth study unit

requirement_groups.title: Foundational studies
requirement_groups.description:

requirement_groups.description: You must complete the following two units.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Studio practices
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 60 credit points comprising:
- DGN1000 Design studio (12 credit points); and
- 48 credit points of specialisation studio units (Collaborative design, Communication design, Industrial design or Spatial design).

requirement_groups.title: Collaborative design
requirement_groups.description:
For this specialisation, you must complete 48 credit points, with at least 36 credit points (three units) from the following list.

Note 1: You must complete Studio 4 and Studio 5 units in your chosen specialisation.
Note 2: You may replace either the Studio 2 or Studio 3 unit below with the equivalent unit from an alternative specialisation.
Note 3: You may replace either the Studio 4 or Studio 5 unit below with the Global design studio unit (DGN3000). Refer to the Global design studio for more information on eligibility.

requirement_groups.title: Global design studio
requirement_groups.description:
Global design studio (DGN3000) can be used to replace Studio 4 (COL3001/CDS3001/IDN3001/SDN3001) or Studio 5 (COL3002/CDS3002/IDN3002/SDN3002) in your chosen specialisation.

DGN3000 is only offered in Prato (Italy) and enrolment is via application and approval only.

requirement_groups.title: Industrial design
requirement_groups.description:
For this specialisation, you must complete 48 credit points, with at least 36 credit points (three units) from the following list.

Note 1: You must complete Studio 4 and Studio 5 units in your chosen specialisation.
Note 2: You may replace either the Studio 2 or Studio 3 unit below with the equivalent unit from an alternative specialisation.
Note 3: You may replace either the Studio 4 or Studio 5 unit below with the Global design studio unit (DGN3000). Refer to the Global design studio for more information on eligibility.

requirement_groups.title: Communication design
requirement_groups.description:
For this specialisation, you must complete 48 credit points, with at least 36 credit points (three units) from the following list.

Note 1: You must complete Studio 4 and Studio 5 units in your chosen specialisation.
Note 2: You may replace either the Studio 2 or Studio 3 unit below with the equivalent unit from an alternative specialisation.
Note 3: You may replace either the Studio 4 or Studio 5 unit below with the Global design studio unit (DGN3000). Refer to the Global design studio for more information on eligibility.

requirement_groups.title: Spatial design
requirement_groups.description:
For this specialisation, you must complete 48 credit points, with at least 36 credit points (three units) from the following list.

Note 1: You must complete Studio 4 and Studio 5 units in your chosen specialisation.

Note 3: You may replace either the Studio 4 or Studio 5 unit below with the Global design studio unit (DGN3000). Refer to the Global design studio for more information on eligibility.

requirement_groups.title: Course rules
requirement_groups.description:
The course comprises 144 credit points, of which 96 credit points are focussed on the study of design and 48 credit points are free electives. Elective units may be at any level, however, no more than 60 credit points are to be completed at Level 1 in the Bachelor of Design.

You must complete 144 credit points comprising:
- 96 credit points of study from your chosen specialisation, either Collaborative design, Communication design, Industrial design or Spatial design ; and
- 48 credit points of free electives.

You must ensure that you complete no more than 10 units (60 credit points) at Level 1 and at least 24 credit points at Level 3 or higher.

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
The course comprises 192 credit points, of which 96 credit points are from the Bachelor of Architectural Studies (including the requirements listed below from Parts A, B and C) and 96 credit points from the Bachelor of Fine Art (including the requirements listed below from Parts A, B and C).

Due to unit BLK1000 being offered in both courses, BLK1000 will be replaced by ADA3012 Transdisciplinary studies in the Bachelor of Architectural Studies. This unit draws together the two disciplines and examines their complementarity/the possibilities that come from having done these two courses together.

In this case, the unit BLK1000 Indigenous Australian creative practice and ways of knowing will be taught as part of the Fine Art programâs 96 core credit points.

F2020.course_title: Bachelor of Design and Bachelor of Fine Art

requirement_groups.title: F2020 - Complete Requirements
requirement_groups.description: Complete all requirements for Bachelor of Design and Bachelor of Fine Art

requirement_groups.title: Course rules
requirement_groups.description:
This double degree includes three main components: common units which are required by both degrees; Design knowledge and practice (critical contexts, disciplinary practices and studio practices); and Fine Art knowledge and practice (critical contexts, disciplinary practices and studio practices).

The respective requirements of each degree have been included in each component to simplify the course requirements for this double degree.

You must complete 192 credit points, comprising:
- 30 credit points of Common units
- 18 credit points of Critical context units
- 126 credit points of studio practices (which includes studio practice for Fine Art, and studio practice for your chosen specialisation in Design)
- 18 credit points of Electives.

requirement_groups.title: Common units
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 30 credit points from the following units.

Note 1: You can complete only one of TDN1002 or AHT1102.
Note 2: You can complete only one of TDN2001 or AHT2102.
Note 3: All students must complete OHS1000 (0 credit point unit).

requirement_groups.title: Electives
requirement_groups.description: You must complete 18 credit points of elective units from Design and/or Fine Art that you have not already completed.

requirement_groups.title: Critical contexts
requirement_groups.description: You must complete the following units

requirement_groups.title: Studio practices
requirement_groups.description: You must complete the Fine Art specialisation and one of the following design specialisations.

requirement_groups.title: Collaborative design specialisation
requirement_groups.description:
For this specialisation, you must complete 60 credit points.

Note 1: All students must complete DGN1000.
Note 2: You may replace either the Studio 2 or Studio 3 unit below with the equivalent unit from an alternative specialisation.
Note 3: You must complete Studio 4 and Studio 5 units in your chosen specialisation.
Note 4: DGN3000 (Global design studio) is an optional unit which you may undertake to replace either the Studio 4 or Studio 5 unit in your specialisation.

requirement_groups.title: Fine Art specialisation
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 66 credit points, comprising:
- 54 credit points of studio practices units;
- 12 credit points of electives.

Note: The 12 credit points of electives may include only Fine Art practical electives units or ADA3406 and one Fine Art practical elective unit.

requirement_groups.title: Industrial design specialisation
requirement_groups.description:
For this specialisation, you must complete 60 credit points.

Note 1: All students must complete DGN1000.
Note 2: You may replace either the Studio 2 or Studio 3 unit below with the equivalent unit from an alternative specialisation.
Note 3: You must complete Studio 4 and Studio 5 units in your chosen specialisation.
Note 4: DGN3000 (Global design studio) is an optional unit which you may undertake to replace either the Studio 4 or Studio 5 unit in your specialisation.

requirement_groups.title: Communication design specialisation

Note 1: All students must complete DGN1000.
Note 2: You may replace either the Studio 2 or Studio 3 unit below with the equivalent unit from an alternative specialisation.

Note 4: DGN3000 (Global design studio) is an optional unit which you may undertake to replace either the Studio 4 or Studio 5 unit in your specialisation.

Note 1: All students must complete DGN1000.
Note 2: You may replace either the Studio 2 or Studio 3 unit below with the equivalent unit from an alternative specialisation.
Note 3: You must complete Studio 4 and Studio 5 units in your chosen specialisation.
Note 4: DGN3000 (Global design studio) is an optional unit which you may undertake to replace either the Studio 4 or Studio 5 unit in your specialisation.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Free electives
requirement_groups.description:
You may take up to six non-law units (36 credit points) provided you satisfy the unit rules.

Note: four non-law units is sufficient to complete a minor in a discipline outside law which would be listed on your transcript. When selecting units to comprise a minor you should refer to the lists of minors in the arts, science, commerce, business and information technology comprehensive courses.

You may be eligible to undertake Flagship Rich Educational Experiences which will be credited towards non-law electives. In eligible double degrees, it may be possible to utilise credit from one or both of the degrees to do a 6 or 12 credit point Flagship Rich Educational Experience unit. Note: the 12 credit point Global Immersion Guarantee may not be available in some double degrees.

If you are completing a double degree course, eight units (48 credit points) required for the partner degree are credited as electives towards the law degree.

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Specified electives
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete between 66 and 102 credit points of law electives which must include at least one law research elective:
- Law research electives may be selected from either the Academic Research or Applied Research elective lists.
- The research elective requirement can also be met by completing a research-integrated postgraduate elective approved for undergraduate enrolment.

Note: High achieving students have the opportunity of undertaking a maximum of two postgraduate electives in their final semester, which can be counted towards a Master of Laws (LLM) degree if they wish to progress to LLM studies.

requirement_groups.title: Applied research electives
requirement_groups.description: Note: These units are available via application to eligible students. Please refer to the Handbook for units for further information.

requirement_groups.title: Academic research electives
requirement_groups.description: Note: LAW4327 Honours thesis is available via application to eligible students. Please refer to the Handbook for units for further information.

requirement_groups.description: You must complete 204 credit points, comprising 102 credit points of core law units and 102 credit points of electives which may include up to 36 credit points of non-law units outside the Faculty of Law or the 48 credit points of non-law units required to be cross credited towards a double degree course.

AND

- one or two Level 2 or Level 3 elective units (6 or 12 credit points) chosen from across Monash University or Faculty of Medicine, Nursing and Health Sciences, including other NUT electives. You must meet any required prerequisites, co-requisites and other enrolment restrictions. You should also be aware of travel and timetabling limitations.

Note: Electives can be identified using the University Handbook search tool. The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code; undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3. You may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties.

requirement_groups.title: M2003 - Complete Requirements
requirement_groups.description: Complete all requirements for Bachelor of Biomedical Science

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Free elective studies
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of free elective study.

Elective units can be chosen from across the Faculty and used to deepen your knowledge of the biomedical sciences. This includes those with the prefixes BCH, BME, DEV, GEN, HUP, IMM, MIC, MIS, NUT, PHA and PHY (please note that some of these are prohibited with core BMS units).

Elective units may also be used to sample disciplines from across the University provided you have met the required prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment into the units. The faculties of Arts, Business and Economics, Engineering, Information Technology and Science offer units particularly suitable as electives.
Electives may be from any level, however, no more than four electives (24 credit points) are to be completed from level 1 and at least two electives (12 credit points) must be completed at Level 3. If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the partner degree are credited as electives towards the biomedical science degree.

Your free electives may be used to study a major (48 credit points) or minor (24 credit points) in a non-biomedical science discipline. This is an optional component of the degree. You cannot do a major or minor in the areas of Biochemistry, Anatomy and developmental biology, Genetics and genomics, Human pathology, Immunology, Microbiology, Pharmacology or Physiology, but you may do individual units from these areas.

requirement_groups.title: Commerce component
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 84 credit points from the Bachelor of Commerce, including 36 credit points from Part A: comprising all core units; 42 credit points in Part B; and 6 credit points in Part C.

Notes:
- The following Bachelor of Commerce majors are not available to the double degree students: Actuarial studies, Business analytics, Finance, and Mathematical foundations of econometrics.
- Prior to commencing Part B, you must complete requirements in Part A as specified above.
- If you wish to become an accredited accountant you should do ECX2953 Economics instead of ECC1000 Principles of microeconomics. If you are seeking membership of ACCA, CA ANZ or CPA you will not be able to complete all the units required to enter their professional programs. However, the units you are unable to complete can be completed after graduation or via professional body courses.

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Professional practice
requirement_groups.description:
Prior to admission to the applied ultrasound practice units (after completion of SON4000 and SON4010), applicants must provide evidence verifying that they are actively engaged in supervised clinical ultrasound practice for the equivalent of at least three days a week. The majority of units within this course are clinically based and you must find yourself a placement as a trainee sonographer. The University does not assist with this process. You need a placement as a trainee sonographer in order to gain the experience that is required to be competent in each of the study areas. We do not recommend that candidates apply for the course unless they are certain that they can secure a clinical placement.

To successfully complete the Master of Medical Ultrasound you will be required to pass clinical competency assessments and have logged a minimum of 2200 hours of supervised clinical sonography experience (general) at a suitable clinic/hospital. Students who do not pass the clinical competency assessments or are unable to complete the required 2,200 hours of supervised clinical sonography experience, will be unable to continue in the master's program. However, they will be eligible to apply to exit with either a Graduate Certificate in Medical Ultrasound (M4003) or a Graduate Diploma of Health Studies (M5015), provided they have met the academic requirements for one of those awards.

You must complete the following units
and
SON5030 Professional accreditation clinical competency assessment (PACCA) (0 credit points)
