requirement_groups.title: S2000 - Complete Requirements
requirement_groups.description: Complete all requirements for Bachelor of Science

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
Note 1: In choosing the 96 credit points of Bachelor of Science listed study over Part A and Part B, you must ensure thatÂ youÂ complete six science listed units (36 credit points) at Level 1, and ten science listed units (60 credit points) at Levels 2 and 3 with a minimum of four units (24 credit points) at Level 3.

The six Level 1 science listed units (36 credit points) include those Level 1 units completed as part of your science specified study.Â You may also chose Level 1 units from any major, extended major or minor within the Bachelor of Scienceâexcept for the business units listed under the Food Science and Business extended major in Malaysia, which are classified as science units only for students enrolled in that specific extended majorâor from Level 1 mathematics and statistics units or from the additional Level 1 science units provided. Similarly, theÂ ten Level 2 and 3 Bachelor of Science listed units (60 credit points) includeÂ the Level 2 and 3 units taken as part of your major in Part B, with at least 4 units (24 credit points) at Level 3. You may also choose Level 2 and 3 units from the list of units under Years two and three, as needed.Â 

Note 2: You must ensure that across the whole course, you complete no more than ten Level 1 units (60 credit points).

Note 3: To progress in the course you must complete the Level 1 sequences at stage one before enrolling in Level 2 science units.

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Science listed major
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete at least one of the following majors.

A major requires eight units with no more than two units at Level 1 (12 credit points) and at least three units (18 credit points) at Level 3. You may replace the major with an extended major by using the elective units available in Part A. or Part C.

It is recommended that you consider completing Level 2 units in more than one area of science to maximise your choice of major at Level 3.

If you are intending to undertake an honours year you should ensure that you complete the specific units required as a prerequisite for honours in your chosen major area of study.

For students undertaking a double degree: Some majors are not available in certain double degrees. Please check the 'Availability' section of each major.

requirement_groups.title: Science extended majors
requirement_groups.description:
You may complete a science extended major, provided you have sufficient room in your course.

You may also use free space in your course to extend your science major to form an extended major.

The Psychology extended major (APAC accredited Level 1 sequence of units) is only offered in the Bachelor of Science (S2000) single degree or in the Bachelor of Science and Bachelor of Arts (S2006) double degree. The Psychology extended major (APAC accredited Level 1 sequence of units) is required if you intend to progress to honours in Psychology.

Please note, science extended majors are not available within the Bachelor of Science (M2021) double degree.

For other students undertaking a double degree: Some extended majors are not available in certain double degrees. Please check the 'Availability' section of each extended major for information.

requirement_groups.title: Science minors
requirement_groups.description:
You may complete a science listed minor provided you have enough room in your course.

Double degree students note: Some minors are not available in certain double degrees, check the Availability section of each minor.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Free elective study
requirement_groups.description:
Elective units may be chosen from the remaining units available in the Bachelor of Science course and will enable you to extend your major or to complete a second major or minor(s).Â 

Elective units may also be taken from non-science disciplines to broaden knowledge or to complete a major or minor(s) from another course as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment in the units.

You may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties.

Note: No more than two units (12 credit points) can be credited towards two majors, or a major and a minor. The same credit points cannot be credited towards more than one minor.

requirement_groups.title: Part A. Science specified study
requirement_groups.description: Although the requirements in Part A appear to exceed 48 points, a level 1 sequence is typically counted towards the chosen major in Part B.

requirement_groups.title: Level 1 science sequences
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete at least two approved Level 1 sequences from the disciplines listed below. The choice of Level 1 sequences will impact your choice of Level 2 and Level 3 units and will normally lay the foundation for a related major. The two Level 1 sequences must be selected from separate disciplines and cannot have units in common.

If you are studying a single degree course, it is recommended that you take a third Level 1 sequence in the first year to increase your options for choice of major. If you are completing a double degree course you will not be able to fit in three Level 1 sequences.

requirement_groups.title: Mathematics and statistics unit
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete at least one of the following Level 1 mathematics or statistics units if not already taken as part of a Level 1 approved sequence. If you have already completed 6 credit points of the following units as part of a level 1 approved sequence, you must replace the Mathematics and statistics unit with a level 1 science unit (6 credit points).

- Please ensure that you have met the pre-requisites for your chosen Level 1 mathematics and statistics unit. For further guidance on your mathematics and statistics unit selection you can refer to the guide provided in the Structure section under Course progression map.
- You must complete this requirement within the first 48 credit points of a single Science course and within the first 72 credit points of a Science combined (double degree) course. For exceptions, please see information on double degree courses below.

requirement_groups.title: Information on double degree courses
requirement_groups.description:
You are exempt from completing the mathematics and statistics unit in the following double degree courses when you complete a corresponding mathematics unit in the requirements of the partner course as listed below:

B2023 Bachelor of Commerce and Bachelor of Science - ETC1000 Business and economic statistics or ETX1100 Business statistics
E3007 Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) and Bachelor of Science - ENG1005 Engineering mathematics

requirement_groups.title: MTH1030 Techniques for modelling
requirement_groups.description:
MTH1030 Techniques for modelling is recommended/required if you have an interest in astrophysics, some streams of climate and atmospheric science, computational science, mathematics and/or physics. MTH1030 requires a raw study score of at least 30 in VCE Specialist Mathematics (or equivalent) or MTH1020.

If you have a strong mathematics background and a keen interest in the subject you could replace the unit MTH1030 with the advanced version MTH1035. You must enrol in this unit manually via the online Enrolment Amendment form.

requirement_groups.title: STA1010 Statistical methods for science
requirement_groups.description: STA1010 Statistical methods for science is recommended if you are planning to study areas where experimental design and data analysis skills are particularly important, such as the life sciences. STA1010 requires VCE Mathematical Methods Units 3 and 4 (or equivalent), or SCI1020 or MTH1010.

requirement_groups.title: MTH1020 Analysis of change
requirement_groups.description: MTH1020 Analysis of change is recommended/ required if you have an interest in astrophysics, some streams of climate and atmospheric science, computational science, mathematics and/or physics. MTH1020 requires a raw study score of at least 25 in VCE Mathematical Methods (or equivalent).

The choice of Level 1 sequences will impact your choice of Level 2 and Level 3 units and will normally lay the foundation for a related major.

If you are studying a single degree course, it is recommended that you take a third Level 1 sequence in the first year to increase your options for choice of major.

requirement_groups.title: Mathematics and statistics unit
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete at least 6 credit points from the following units, if not already taken as part of a level 1 approved sequence. If you have already completed 6 credit points of the following units as part of a level 1 approved sequence, you must replace the Mathematics and statistics unit with a level 1 science unit (6 credit points).

- The unit you choose will depend on your mathematics background and interests (see information on units below.)
- You must complete this within the first 48 credit points of your course.
- It is in your best interest to ensure that you have met pre-requisites for your chosen level 1 mathematics and statistics unit.
- For further guidance on your mathematics and statistics unit selection you can refer to the guide provided in the Structure section under Course progression map.

requirement_groups.title: MTH1030 Techniques for modelling
requirement_groups.description: MTH1030 Techniques for modelling is recommended if you have an interest in mathematics and/or physics. MTH1030 requires you to have studied VCE Specialist Mathematics (or equivalent) or MTH1020. If you have a strong mathematics background and a keen interest in the subject you could replace the unit MTH1030 with the advanced version MTH1035. You will need to seek permission from ask.monash to enrol in this unit.

requirement_groups.title: SCI1020 Introduction to statistical reasoning
requirement_groups.description: SCI1020 Introduction to statistical reasoning is recommended if you are planning to study areas where experimental design and data analysis skills are particularly important, such as the life sciences. SCI1020 is for students without a strong mathematics background.

requirement_groups.title: STA1010 Statistical methods for science
requirement_groups.description: STA1010 Statistical methods for science is recommended if you are planning to study areas where experimental design and data analysis skills are particularly important, such as the life sciences. STA1010 requires you to have studied VCE Mathematical Methods Units 3 and 4 (or equivalent).

requirement_groups.title: MTH1020 Analysis of change
requirement_groups.description: MTH1020 Analysis of change is recommended if you have an interest in mathematics and/or physics. MTH1020 requires you to have studied VCE Mathematical Methods (or equivalent).

requirement_groups.title: SCI1022 Introduction to scientific coding
requirement_groups.description: SCI1022 Introduction to scientific coding is recommended if you want to gain fundamental coding skills that can be applied in a variety of scientific areas. It will be useful for data analysis, modelling of complex systems, data manipulation and visualisation in a scientific context. SCI1022 requires you to have studied VCE Mathematical Methods Units 3 and 4 (or equivalent) or STA1010.

requirement_groups.title: Additional level 2 or 3 science units
requirement_groups.description:
You may complete as many additional Level 2 or 3 science units as necessary to take the total of science listed units to 78 credit points over Parts A and B.

Science listed units are chosen from units listed under any major, extended major or minor offered in S2000 Bachelor of Science or from the following units.

You must complete SCI3501 prior to taking SCI3920.

Note that, for the purpose of this course, MTE4235 is counted as a Level 3 science elective and cannot be used towards the requirement of the 4th year of the course.

requirement_groups.title: Note 1: Unit selection
requirement_groups.description: In selecting the 126 credit points of science listed study across Parts A and B, you must ensure that you complete 42 credit points at Level 1 and 72 credit points at Levels 2 and 3, with a minimum of 30 credit points at Level 3. You must also ensure that across the entire course, you complete no more than 60 credit points at Level 1. It is recommended that you complete level 1 sequences first, as they provide essential foundations for later study.

requirement_groups.title: Note 3: Fourth year eligibility, internship and exit award
requirement_groups.description:
To progress to the fourth year, you must complete 144 credit points, including all six âImpact through Scienceâ units from Levels 1 to 3. You are also required to complete an internship as part of SCI3501 Impact through Science 3A (6 credit points), which should be completed before Semester 2 of your third year. You must also achieve a distinction average (70%) in 18 credit points of Level 3 units within your science discipline and in the Level 3 âImpact through Scienceâ units.

If you complete 144 credit points but do not meet the academic standard for fourth-year entry, you will graduate with a Bachelor of Science.

requirement_groups.title: Note 2: Academic requirement
requirement_groups.description: To remain in the course, you must maintain a distinction average (70%) across all units each calendar year. Failure to meet this requirement will result in a transfer to S2000, the Bachelor of Science.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Impact through science project
requirement_groups.description: In your final year you must complete the following units

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Science listed major
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete a Bachelor of Science major.

A major requires eight units with no more than 12 credit points at Level 1 and at least 18 credit points at Level 3.

You may replace the major with an extended major by using elective units available in Part A or Part C.

requirement_groups.title: Science minors
requirement_groups.description: You may use free space in your course to complete a science minor in addition to your science major.

requirement_groups.title: Science extended majors
requirement_groups.description: You may use free space in your course to complete a science extended major or to extend your science major to form an extended major.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Free elective study
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 18 credit points of free elective study.

These elective units may be chosen from units available in the Bachelor of Science course to broaden your science knowledge or to extend your major.

Elective units may also be taken from non-science disciplines as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment in the units.

Free electives can be identified using the Advanced search tool in the current edition of the Handbook. The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code; undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3.

You may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties.

You cannot take SCI1000 within this course.

requirement_groups.title: Note 1: Unit selection
requirement_groups.description:
In choosing your 126 credit points of science listed study over Part A and Part B, you must ensure that you complete six science listed units (36 credit points) at Level 1 and ten science listed units (60 credit points) at Levels 2 and 3, with a minimum of four units (24 credit points) at Level 3.

You must also ensure that across the whole course, you complete no more than ten units (60 credit points) at Level 1. It is recommended that you complete Level 1 sequences early, as they provide essential foundations for further study.

If you're a first-year student considering enrolling in Level 2 units, it is recommended that you consult your course coordinator to explore suitable options.

requirement_groups.title: Note 3: Fourth year eligibility, internship and exit award
requirement_groups.description:
To progress to the fourth year, you must complete 144 credit points. You must also achieve a distinction average (70%) in 24 credit points of Level 3 units in your intended honours discipline.

If you complete 144 credit points but do not meet the academic standard for fourth-year entry, you will graduate with a Bachelor of Science.

requirement_groups.title: Note 2: Academic requirement
requirement_groups.description: To remain in the course, you must maintain a distinction average (70%) across all science units each calendar year. Failure to meet this requirement will result in a transfer to S2000, the Bachelor of Science.

requirement_groups.title: Part D. Honours program
requirement_groups.description:
The specific requirements for the honours year are dependent on your field of study. All units will be at Level 4 and in general will include advanced coursework and specified assessment tasks, a thesis component and seminars or oral presentations.

Details of the units of study are in accordance with the requirements for course S3701 Bachelor of Science (Honours).

requirement_groups.title: Part B. Science listed major
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete two science listed majors or an extended major plus a minor in a different area of study.

A major requires eight units with no more than two units at Level 1 (12 credit points), and at least three units (18 credit points) at Level 3.

An extended major requires twelve units with no more than four units at Level 1 (24 credit points) and at least four units (24 credit points) at Level 3.

You should ensure that over the course you have completed at least four units (24 credit points) of science listed study at Level 3.

It is recommended that you consider completing Level 2 units in more than one area of science to maximise your choice of major at Level 3.

You should ensure you complete the specific units required for honours in your chosen major.

requirement_groups.title: Part A. Science specified study
requirement_groups.description: Although the requirements in Part A appear to exceed 24 credit points, the two Level 1 sequences are typically credited towards your majors or minor (Part B) and not towards this Part.

requirement_groups.title: Mathematics and statistics unit
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete at least one of the following Level 1 mathematics and statistics units (6 credit points) if not already taken as part of a Level 1 approved sequence. If you have already completed 6 credit points of the following units as part of a level 1 approved sequence, you must replace the mathematics and statistics unit with a level 1 science unit (6 credit points).

The unit you choose will depend on your mathematics background and interests (see information on units below). You must complete this within the first 48 credit points of your course. It is in your best interest to ensure that you have met the prerequisites for your chosen Level 1 mathematics and statistics unit. For further guidance on your mathematics and statistics unit selection you can refer to the guide provided in the Structure section under Course progression map.

requirement_groups.title: MTH1030 Techniques for modelling
requirement_groups.description: MTH1030 is recommended if you have an interest in mathematics and/or physics. MTH1030 requires you to have studied VCE Specialist Mathematics (or equivalent) or MTH1020. If you have a strong mathematics background and a keen interest in the subject you could replace MTH1030 with the advanced version MTH1035. You will need to seek permission from ask.monash to enrol in this unit.

requirement_groups.title: STA1010 Statistical methods for science
requirement_groups.description: STA1010 is recommended if you plan to study areas where experimental design and data analysis skills are particularly important, such as the life sciences. STA1010 requires you to have studied VCE Mathematical Methods Units 3 and 4 (or equivalent).

requirement_groups.title: MTH1020 Analysis of change
requirement_groups.description: MTH1020 is recommended if you have an interest in mathematics and/or physics. MTH1020 requires you to have studied VCE Mathematical Methods (or equivalent).

requirement_groups.title: SCI1022 Introduction to scientific coding
requirement_groups.description: SCI1022 is recommended if you want to gain fundamental coding skills that can be applied in a variety of scientific areas. It will be useful for data analysis, modelling of complex systems, data manipulation and visualisation in a scientific context. SCI1022 requires you to have studied VCE Mathematical Methods Units 3 and 4 (or equivalent) or STA1010.

Note: The research project unit is normally taken as part of your chosen major(s). The computational science project is 12 credit points and consists of FIT3161 and FIT3162. These units do not form part of the computational science major but are part of the extended major.

If you major in computational science and want to complete FIT3161 and FIT3162 as your level three research project course requirement, then they will not count towards your major in computational science.

requirement_groups.title: Additional level 2 or 3 science unit
requirement_groups.description:
You may complete any additional Level 2 or Level 3 science units required to take the total science listed units to 126 credit points over Parts A and Part B.

Science listed units may be chosen from the units below or units listed under any major, extended major or minor offered in the S2000 Bachelor of Science.

Note that, for the purpose of this course, MTE4235 is counted as a level 3 science elective and cannot be used towards the requirement of the 4th year of the course.

requirement_groups.title: Level 1 science sequences
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete at least two approved Level 1 sequences from the disciplines listed below.

The choice of Level 1 sequences will impact your choice of Level 2 and Level 3 units and will normally lay the foundation for a related major. The two Level 1 sequences must be selected from separate disciplines and cannot have units in common.

If you are studying a single degree course, it is recommended that you take a third Level 1 sequence in the first year to increase your options for choice of major.

If you are a first-year student and wish to take Level 2 units you should discuss options with your course coordinator.

requirement_groups.title: Part C. Free elective study
requirement_groups.description:
You may choose elective units from units available in the S2000 Bachelor of Science course to broaden your science knowledge or to extend your major.

Elective units may also be taken from non-science disciplines as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment in the units. The level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code; undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3.

You may need permission from the owning faculty to enrol in some units taught by other faculties.

You cannot take SCI1020 and MTH1010 within this course.

requirement_groups.title: Rules
requirement_groups.description:
The course comprises 192 credit points of which 168 credit points are focussed on Applied Data Science. No more than 10 units (60 credit points) are to be completed at Level 1 in the course.

PROGRESSION TO FOURTH YEAR: In order to progress to the fourth year, you must normally complete 144 credit points comprising 132 credit points as indicated in parts A, B and C and 12 credit points from Part E. You must also achieve a minimum of a distinction average (70%) in 36 credit points of core Level 3 units. If you do not meet the course requirements you will be awarded the Bachelor of Applied Data Science.

requirement_groups.title: Part E. Free elective study
requirement_groups.description:
You must complete 24 credit points of electives.

- 12 credit points of electives completed during Years 1â3.
- 12 credit points of electives at Level 3 or higher, completed in Year 4 with the approval of the course director.

Elective units may be chosen from Parts B or C of the course, not already completed, or from across the University, as long as you meet the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolment. Free electives can be identified using the Advanced Search tool in the current edition of the Handbook. The level of a unit is indicated by the first digit of its unit code; undergraduate units begin with the numbers 1, 2 or 3. Please note that you may require permission from the owning faculty to enrol in units offered by other faculties.

Twelve (12) credit points of elective units in your Honours year must be at Level 3 or higher, and approved for Year 4 by the Course Director.
